title: fagraea acutibracteata
intro: Fagraea acutibracteata, also known as the wild gardenia or the fragrant gardenia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. This plant is traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Fagraea acutibracteata include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and improve digestion. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
